description: ESPN 'HAI @ BRA' kicks off 00:30Z (next UTC day) but Polymarket dates the slug to the prior US-Eastern day; home-away slug order; three legs home/away/draw.

polymarket.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Friday, June 19, 2026 between Brazil and Haiti.

eventMetadata.context_description: Brazil enters the 2026 FIFA World Cup Group C clash against Haiti as overwhelming favorites, reflecting the vast gulf in squad depth, technical quality, and historical dominance—the Seleção have won all prior meetings by an aggregate of 17-1. Coming off a draw with Morocco that exposed attacking inconsistencies, Brazil under Carlo Ancelotti will likely deploy Vinícius Júnior, Raphinha, and Endrick to exploit Haiti’s defensive vulnerabilities after Les Grenadiers conceded in their opener versus Scotland. Haiti’s second-ever World Cup appearance and limited recent form underscore the matchup mismatch at Lincoln Financial Field. Realistic challenges remain limited to a disciplined Haitian defensive stand, set-piece fortune, or a rare Brazilian off-night, though the implied probability aligns with the talent and experience disparity.

markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 19, 2026
If Brazil wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
markets.question: Will Brazil win on 2026-06-19?
